# Operator Interaction

Phase: Common

Print operator-facing messages and collect explicit local operator decisions or values.

## Summary

- phase: `common`
- group: `operator-interaction`
- kinds: [Message](#message)

## When To Use This Group

Use this group when a workflow needs a clear local checkpoint or an apply-time value that should flow through register outputs.

## Typical Flows

### Gate a local action

- kinds: [Message](#message)

## Shared Step Fields

Shared step envelope fields such as `id`, `apiVersion`, `kind`, `when`, `parallelGroup`, `retry`, `timeout`, `register`, and `metadata` are documented in [Step Envelope Contract](../../workflow-model.md#step-envelope-contract).

## `Message`

Print an operator-facing message during workflow execution.


### When To Use

Use this to explain a manual checkpoint, show rendered context, or make an apply/prepare workflow self-describing without shell commands.

### Example

```yaml
kind: Message
spec:
  level: info
  message: |
    Node setup is starting.
    role: {{ .vars.role }}
```

### Spec Fields

| Key | Type | Required | Default | Enum | Description | Example |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| `spec.level` | `string` | no | `` | `info, warn, error` | Message severity used as a human-readable prefix for warnings and errors. Defaults to `info`. | `info` |
| `spec.message` | `string` | yes | `` | `` | Message text to print. Templates are rendered before the step runs and YAML block scalars support multi-line text. | `Node setup is starting.` |
| `spec.stream` | `string` | no | `` | `stdout, stderr` | Output stream for the message. Defaults to `stdout`. | `stdout` |

### Notes

- `Message` is intended for human-readable operator output, not machine-readable workflow data.
- Do not use `Message` to display secrets.
